[](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d6339a6/1a8421e1/1a4231417/4b3ff9b7/3537693547/VEsH/ "http://www.tomohalleran.com")

Friends, Here at Team O'Halleran, we've been calling on Donald Trump's corrupt chief of the Environmental Protection Agency, Scott Pruitt, to resign for weeks now. _Well, this seems like a good time to double down, because he 's still refusing to resign -- even as even MORE scandals are piling up around him._ [**SIGN ON: Scott Pruitt needs to go >>**](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d6339a6/1a8421e1/1a4231417/4b3ff9b8/3537693547/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg1MzA3In0=/ "http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/pruitt-resign?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=85307") _Take a look at the latest headline:_ **Pruitt spent at least $9,600 on office decor, desks** _" The expenses listed for Pruitt appear to surpass the $5,000 limit set by Congress for redecoration costs"_ _\- The Hill _ What's amazing is that Pruitt spending too much of your hard-earned, taxpayer dollars on office furniture doesn't even begin to top the list of his worst abuses. Pruitt has been under fire**for months** for allegedly misusing taxpayer dollars for exorbitant personal travel and other expenses -- and then demoting or firing aides who raised concerns about it. There are few issues Tom is more passionate about than making sure our government is transparent and accountable. Well, Scott Pruitt is not transparent, and he has not been held accountable. [**We need to change that: Join Tom in calling on Scott Pruitt to resign TODAY >>**](http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/m/1d6339a6/1a8421e1/1a4231417/4b3ff9b8/3537693547/VEsF/p/eyJKU1ZGYldGcGJDVWwiOiJkcHdpbGxpczY3QGdtYWlsLmNvbSIsIkpTVkdhWEp6ZEc1aGJXVWxKUT09IjoiIiwiSlNWTVlYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IiIsIkpTVmFhWEFsSlE9PSI6Ijg1MzA3In0=/ "http://action.tomohalleran.com/page/s/pruitt-resign?email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&firstname=&lastname=&zip=85307") Thanks, Team O'Halleran | ****[DONATE](http://action.tomohalleran. name: Paul Davis
subject: More respect than we can give
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Paul Davis for Kansas
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/n2B-LjoV-YHRLlfjYme0EhAB0CfH68_5AYjcgY9T29Y=/-ANeBmJCE7Uxd5u2yndvlWFnuP-mzniaiKIh-QdsJUM=)
Dear Peter,

On Memorial Day, we honor and remember all of those who fought bravely to keep us safe, protect our freedom, and who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country along the way.

The bravery represented by all of the men and women who join our armed forces is unparalleled -- but those who have given their lives in service to our country deserve more respect than we could ever give them.

Today, take a moment to honor all of the brave members of our armed forces who gave their lives to ensure that we were able to live ours freely.

Happy Memorial Day,

Paul Davis

name: Mary Barzee Flores for Congress
subject: Have a good Memorial Day Captain Mueller
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Mary Barzee Flores for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/ktcGuNxFzhWxGvdFwtBc3mpIU_Pkqtus-YOhUkAPQ5s=/ax-4li19h9cmKB6f99b4UEbiG4gAciq92Xz0F1Pu9oE=)

Friend,
**In his last Memorial Day as our 44th Commander-in-Chief, President Barack Obama tweeted, "I hope you'll join me in acts of remembrance. The debt we owe our fallen heroes is one we can never truly repay."**
We would do well to follow President Obama's lead and honor the memory of those who made the ultimate sacrifice on behalf of our great nation.
And while I'm thanking those brave men and women who put their lives on the line for the rest of us, I'd like offer a special salute to a living American hero who served as a Captain in the Marine Corps in Vietnam, came home with a Bronze star and a Purple Heart, and dedicated his life to public service.
Happy Memorial Day, Captain Mueller.
Thank you for your sacrifice,
**\- Mary**

Paid for by Mary Barzee Flores for Congress

Friends --

Memorial Day is dedicated to the memory of those who have fallen to protect our freedoms. And today, we stop and remember the men and women who gave the ultimate sacrifice to keep us safe, as well as their families and their loved ones.

Wishing everyone a safe Memorial Day.

Thank you,

Andrew

name: Jason Yates
subject: We remember those who paid the ultimate sacrifice
body:
| | The truths we must remember today
---
| Peter, Memorial Day for many people is spent at barbecues and spending time with friends and family but to others this day is solemn. It’s a day to honor and remember those who died in service to our nation, men and women who paid the ultimate price so that you and I can live free. Memorial Day was declared an official federal holiday in 1971, but it has been unofficially celebrated since the end of the Civil War, when many held springtime tributes to fallen soldiers, decorating their graves and reciting prayers. A remarkable Memorial Day Speech delivered by Oliver Wendell Holmes Jr, a Civil War Veteran who later went on to serve on the U.S. Supreme Court, said this on Memorial Day in 1884. "So to the indifferent inquirer who asks why Memorial Day is still kept up we may answer, it celebrates and solemnly reaffirms from year to year a national act of enthusiasm and faith. It embodies in the most impressive form our belief that to act with enthusiasm and faith is the condition of acting greatly." Oliver Wendell Holmes Jr. is remembered for a speech he delivered on Memorial Day entitled, “In Our Youth Our Hearts Were Touched With Fire.” As we remember those who paid the ultimate price of freedom today and express our deepest gratitude to their loved ones, we also reflect on these words from Holmes. "But grief is not the end of all. I seem to hear the funeral march become a paean. I see beyond the forest the moving banners of a hidden column. Our dead brothers still live for us, and bid us think of life, not death – of life to which in their youth they lent the passion and joy of the spring. As I listen, the great chorus of life and joy begins again, and amid the awful orchestra of seen and unseen powers and destinies of good and evil our trumpets sound once more a note of daring, hope, and will." Today, we honor all the fallen soldiers who have laid down their lives for our country and we reflect on the great chorus of life and freedom their sacrifice has bestowed upon us. We also remember the ultimate example of perfect sacrifice was given to us by Jesus Christ when he laid down his life for our sins. John 15:13 says, “Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.” Christ was the ultimate example of sacrifice and those who have died in service to our nation remind us of that truth. The life we enjoy here in America is made possible because of sacrifices made by our men and women in uniform! We are indebted to those who laid down their lives for us, to see our nation free and safe; and we honor the families who forever deeply feel the great sacrifices that were made. We sincerely thank you and remember and honor your loved ones today. **United. We Stand.** Jason Yates
CEO

name: Dan Lipinski
subject: Remember
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Dan Lipinski for Congress
body:
[](https://act.myngp.com/el/jBoZeZNWkpircElN1NxxOxl5OcfebSN7_Re8K0IgzFc=/kSjp-X2Ypc2tc4dVXn7BbTh6Pdcdl0lbJtJZZY3OGbk=)
Peter,

For many Americans, Memorial Day is a day off of work or school. While I hope we all spend some time with our families, I also hope that we'll spend today reflecting on the brave heroes in our armed forces that have given their lives over the centuries so we can enjoy this beautiful country.

Today, and every day, let's remember those honorable men and women who've made the ultimate sacrifice.

Thanks,

Dan

name: Joe
subject: On Memorial Day
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Donnelly for Indiana
body:
Today, Americans across the country remember and honor the men and women who have died fighting for our freedoms. |
---

| | Peter,

Today, Americans across the country remember and honor the men and women who have died fighting for our freedoms.

I hope you'll take the opportunity today to thank our Gold Star families, veterans, and active duty service members, but most importantly take a moment to reflect on those who paid the ultimate price to protect our country. In the Senate there's nothing that means more to me than my responsibility to advocate for policies that keep our troops safe while they are serving our country.

Those who have served have put their lives on the line to protect our freedom and our flag. They have gone to serve in Korea and Vietnam, Afghanistan and Iraq, knowing they may not come home again. We must honor the commitments we've made to them, both while they wear the uniform and long after they return home.

Wishing you a safe and reflective Memorial Day,
Joe |

body:
On this Memorial Day we remember and honor those who made the ultimate sacrifice. | | | | [ ](https://electroza.us15.list-manage.com/track/click?u=48401e7aa1559c6f628bec1bd&id=b16e1d2367&e=3c6155f94b)
---
| | |
---
|
---
| Today we remember the women and men who died while serving in the armed forces. Those who signed up to fight for America, vowed to protect the rights and freedoms guaranteed to us by the Constitution.

If you've ever had the privilege to visit the Arlington National Cemetery in Virginia, or the Gettysburg National Cemetery in Pennsylvania, you recognize that it is not enough just to remember, but we must also honor their sacrifice.
---
|
---
---
|
---
The Tomb of the Unknown Soldier is guarded 24 hours a day, 365 days a year in any weather by Tomb Guard sentinels _(Arlington National Cemetery, Arlington, VA)._
|
---
| We honor them by continuing to fight for _**our**_ America — one that allows its citizens to thrive and is the leader charging its way towards a better tomorrow.
* We honor them by taking care of our veterans, those who are currently serving and their families.
* We honor them by pursuing equality for all and not settling for anything less.
* We honor them by restoring the promise of democracy.
* We honor them by promoting peace and diplomacy.

We do these things so that the sacrifices of these brave men and women who gave their lives on behalf of our nation, did not do so in vain.
---
|
---
| We must always remember that the cost of war is more than just a budget sheet. The cost of war is also the price of taking care of our men and women when they get home and their families when they don’t come home.

Our veterans fulfilled their duty by defending our nation wherever and whenever necessary — will you join me in ensuring that our nation also budgets to care for them when they return?

subject: A Better Senator Than Me...
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY COURTLAND SYKES FOR SENATE
body:
| | | |
---
| | | **Peter,**

**A BETTER SENATOR THAN I**

We remember many loved ones this Memorial Day Weekend. Today, I reflect on a former colleague and friend. He would have made for a worthier senator than I, and I want to introduce you to the person he was.

Brendan Looney and I served together in Iraq. After graduating from the U.S. Naval Academy, Brendan served as an Intelligence Officer with Navy Special Warfare before becoming a Navy SEAL.

While working with Brendan, I learned lessons that will serve Missouri well should I earn the honor of being your next U.S. Senator.

**Listening to the Wisdom of your Team**
Brendan was always good about placing his ego in his back pocket. He might have been the smartest and hardest working member of the team, but he was always respectful of others' views. The final outcome was always the top priority.

**Service Before Self**
Brendan understood that service meant placing those on your team before yourself. Service means working with difficult people, or people who find you difficult, in order to reach a successful goal.

**Never Get Outworked**
Brendan refused to be outworked. Brendan Inspired everyone around him to work harder. Through his work ethic he taught us, there is always more to give; there is always more to do.

Brendan made the ultimate sacrifice for this nation. Since Brendan is not here, the responsibility of service falls on our shoulders. We all have someone we are carrying the torch for each day. Everyday we are fighting for our families, our society, our faith.

When I'm faced with challenges in this race, to earn the nomination to run against Claire here in Missouri, I think about Brendan. I can't give up. I can't stop fighting to restore this Constitutional Republic. Brendan is no longer here to do it with us. We must carry the torch for those who have passed and those who have come before us.

Now, our Constitutional Republic is under threat; not just by a foreign entity, but by the deep state in Washington who are working night and day against President Trump and the American people.

When I enlisted in the U.S. Navy, I pledged to defend the Constitution with my life against enemies foreign and domestic. I didn't understand the nature of the domestic enemy until I entered the world of politics. I'm fighting establishment (domestic) enemies everyday who are creating an autocratic government, working to eliminate your vote, and enemies who are keeping true patriots from serving in Washington. My torch is to take Brendan's passion for our country, his honor, and his leadership to clean up the quagmire in D.C.

Let's carry this torch together -- we must continue the fight, we must remember those who have come before us, and those who have made today possible. We can fight alone, but together we prevail. Here's to our fight.
[Stand with me.](https://sykesforsenate2018.us17.list-manage.com/track/click?u=4cc1eaa615d77dd655322ba1d&id=832559be0d&e=9da9b0ca31)

Courtland Sykes

Peter,

Growing up in Union City, my parents taught me the value of hard work and the importance of serving our community and country.

**I also learned the value of the dollar.** That’s why I don’t take it lightly when I ask our strongest supporters for a donation.

This campaign to take back New Jersey and restore New Jersey’s trust in their Senator will rely on small donations from New Jerseyans like YOU from all over the state. Supporters like you believe in my message of putting New Jerseyans first.

_As a Marine veteran, I know I can’t go it alone. I need your help to put New Jerseyans first._

**[>> Chip in just $6 to join me in my fight to make sure we elect a Senator the Garden State can be proud of <<](http://links.bobhugin.com/u/click?_t=e73291e89afe41a2b14d8f6ad40d5630&_m=45ba0bffb75b4d7ebf558ff4e47ea035&_e=FJX1uG3Frd2d3Eh8zzEvMoq9xld_2877_YO4soT93Utserji_N1nLI7n32QgGjxSTo-qtCmPvz7Uyf_Ey9SqLer4VM3t7OJMbzLjiVW8kbmWWIv4xldRlelCCzmq52GL02o86MMti-HFzBHar2wbIUsHWwWA9OuFgtSVnDAV3PdDq2-CswagHzHg-vLQVSmude3kTiuN9sTrnSVtRTeYKCO65mRvDtlUhKyDfHytOQ531W5QLGH1A195YdhwG40EgrzrtOZe3XL_rCHyAItjHWT7BiJaNCJMY6NkXA6nVOTc9qT00OKY7viVfnUU8c4ekuHcTHaZkN9sVx-L_M0z6lYOS5fVHXoKnQSRZRySD-YX8ewc5oJiTXFY-rc1ypK3g_Awtub-2V5TXAF_qeeHyWwshm8tTRJhZ5dJBl2Zc2s%3D)**

**I’m running to ensure that the opportunity to live the American Dream is available for future generations growing up in every single community in our great state.**

Donating even just one dollar means that you’re investing in the future of New Jersey, and helping make sure we leave no New Jerseyans behind.

**[>> Whether it’s $10 or $100, we need your help – donate any amount now <<](http://links.bobhugin.com/u/click?_t=e73291e89afe41a2b14d8f6ad40d5630&_m=45ba0bffb75b4d7ebf558ff4e47ea035&_e=FJX1uG3Frd2d3Eh8zzEvMoq9xld_2877_YO4soT93Utserji_N1nLI7n32QgGjxSTo-qtCmPvz7Uyf_Ey9SqLer4VM3t7OJMbzLjiVW8kbmWWIv4xldRlelCCzmq52GL02o86MMti-HFzBHar2wbIUsHWwWA9OuFgtSVnDAV3PdDq2-CswagHzHg-vLQVSmude3kTiuN9sTrnSVtRTeYKKm5_SofTkV4gzoXn9qaNyLfkfOnEX2I3jobd5fDsTXynsfbVcfMDw9fwhHUseUkbL6H5a0pLjvGNz0Bux6hr0O5vmNuHkxTY1zYvCT5TItZTOBA1jsu7QdV6nOd2MnFrbQplrUEllt86SAaTDCX1dEPMl1KTjWIKIWnTSvknploYpQP2saot1-3ZSx_qefv6N7ISnfSA6VFnHZ8LfbDKvs%3D)**

Hey there --

All the experts are saying that Democrats have a chance to win Congress, governors' seats, and local races across the country in a blue wave this election year.
